entries.title: Scanner FP category: test files
entries.description: Security test files (tests/, __tests__/) contain attack payloads as fixtures. Scanner correctly finds patterns but they're in test context.

entries.title: Scanner FP category: minified JS
entries.description: String.fromCharCode in minified/bundled JS, hex_escape in vendor files. All false positives for obfuscation.

entries.title: Scanner FP category: shields.io badges
entries.description: data:image/svg+xml;base64 in README badge URLs triggers markdown_injection. Fixed with URL-start-only matching.

entries.title: Scanner skip rule: security detectors / judge repos
entries.description: Security scanners, judges, and classifiers contain injection vocabulary they detect (e.g., 'ignore previous instructions' in training data). These produce injection FP hits. NOT the same as Cat 9 auto-clear — this is a scanner-level skip for known classifier repos. Cat 9 auto-clear handles the broader monorepo case via inj-to-total ratio.

entries.title: Scanner FP category: CJK unicode
entries.description: Chinese/Japanese/Korean text uses unicode escape sequences. Threshold raised from 3 to 6 consecutive escapes.

entries.title: Scanner FP category: webpack chunks
entries.description: Bundled frontend assets (mermaid, KaTeX) contain hex_escape and fromCharCode. Cat 10 skip for chunk- files.

entries.title: Scanner FP category: protobuf
entries.description: Protobuf generated _pb2.py files contain binary descriptors that trigger obfuscation. Skip rule added.

entries.title: Scanner FP category: .claude/commands
entries.description: .claude/commands/*.md imperative text triggers injection scanner. New FP pattern discovered Run 2.

entries.title: Structural FP: full-repo skills (SkillsMP)
entries.description: SkillsMP skills link to full project repos (e.g., jetbrains/intellij-community 182K files). Scanner scans entire codebase, not just the skill. Not a pattern accuracy issue — structural input issue. Handled by Cat 9 (monorepo ratio) and Cat 10 (PM-verified orgs) auto-clear.

entries.title: Company policy: no false detections
entries.description: False detections are NOT allowed. Scanner regex patterns must be tested with both real attacks AND common legitimate patterns before deployment.

entries.title: Override_role context requirement
entries.description: Pattern requires instruction verb prefix (you must, please, now) before OVERRIDE ROLE/MODE/SYSTEM.

entries.title: Roleplay_escape trailing requirement
entries.description: Pattern requires MODE/NOW/IMMEDIATELY after EXIT/END/LEAVE ROLEPLAY/CHARACTER.

entries.title: Bug FIXED: empty scan_report with findings in agent_audit
entries.description: RESOLVED. Scanner produced findings (in agent_c_star audit) but scan_report field was empty {}. Auto-clear could not inspect findings. Fixed in run_verify_strict_5agent.py: build_scan_report() added (line 809), called at line 1117, written at line 1142. Affected 637 skills in Batch 9 before fix.

entries.title: Scanner FP: Python data-URI f-strings
entries.description: f'![{alt}](data:{mime};base64,{data})' triggers regex_markdown_injection. Legitimate image embedding for PDF/API. Affects 2 overrides.
entries.action: Exclude data: in f-string interpolation from markdown_injection pattern

entries.title: TRULY_DANGEROUS_OBF: the three real obfuscation threats
entries.description: Only three obfuscation rule_ids are considered truly dangerous: regex_py_rot13, regex_py_marshal_loads, regex_py_chr_concat. All other obfuscation findings (hex_escape_seq, unicode_escape_seq, webpack chunks, protobuf) are scanner noise. This distinction drives the hard-block rule and Cat 2/3 auto-clear behavior.

entries.title: Hard-block rule: BOTH dangerous_obf AND injection required
entries.description: Auto-clear is blocked ONLY when dangerous_obf_count > 0 AND injection_patterns_count > 0 simultaneously. Rationale: real attacks combine obfuscation (to hide payload) with injection (to deliver it). Obfuscation alone, or injection alone, is insufficient to be a real threat. This rule is evaluated BEFORE all category checks.
entries.code_ref: run_verify_strict_5agent.py line 126: if dangerous_obf_count > 0 and inj > 0: return None, None, None
entries.evidence: 600+ PM overrides confirmed: zero real attacks with obf-only or inj-only pattern

entries.title: Auto-clear Cat 1: clean profile scoring ceiling
entries.description: Condition: inj == 0 AND obf_hr == 0. Result: pass, score = max(50, current_score). Handles skills stuck in MR/fail purely due to scoring formula edge cases with genuinely clean profiles. Evaluated first after hard-block check.

entries.title: Auto-clear Cat 2: obfuscation-only (0 injection)
entries.description: Condition: obf_hr > 0 AND inj == 0. Result: pass. Handles ALL obf-only cases regardless of file count — including cases with dangerous obf patterns (rot13/marshal/chr_concat) because without injection there is no delivery mechanism. Note: Cat 3 in code (obf + large repo) is dead code — Cat 2 already covers it. Evidence: 134+ PM overrides, all obf-only, zero real attacks.
entries.important_nuance: Cat 2 clears even dangerous_obf_count > 0 when inj == 0. Hard-block only fires when BOTH are present.

entries.title: Auto-clear Cat 3: large-repo obfuscation (dead code — subsumed by Cat 2)
entries.description: Condition: obf_hr > 0 AND inj == 0 AND files_scanned >= 200. This is dead code — Cat 2 (obf_hr > 0 AND inj == 0) fires first and has no file-count requirement. Cat 3 can never be reached. Noted here so SecM does not add a files_scanned requirement to Cat 2 thinking Cat 3 handles large repos separately.

entries.title: Auto-clear Cat 10: PM-verified organizations
entries.description: Condition: GitHub org extracted from repo_url is in PM_VERIFIED_ORGS frozenset. Result: pass regardless of inj or obf counts. 47 orgs as of 2026-03-04. These are internet-verified major OSS/enterprise orgs where security context is established. Handles both injection AND obfuscation FPs for trusted sources.

entries.title: Auto-clear Cat 9: large monorepo injection ratio
entries.description: Condition: total_findings >= 500 AND inj <= 49 (injection ratio < 10% of all findings). Result: pass. Handles full-project repos (SkillsMP structural issue) where injection patterns are buried in a sea of other findings. Injection buried in large codebase is scanner noise, not a targeted attack.
entries.note: This is NOT the same as the scanner-level skip for security detector repos (secm-p-004). Different mechanism, different scope.

entries.title: Auto-clear Cat 11: incidental low-count injection
entries.description: Condition: inj <= 8 AND total_findings < 500. Result: pass. Handles skills where a small number of injection pattern matches appear in small-to-medium repos — not enough signal to indicate intentional malicious injection. Evidence: patterns like hidden_instruction match legitimate imperative documentation.

entries.title: Auto-clear Cat 12: medium injection without dangerous obfuscation
entries.description: Condition: inj <= 49 AND dangerous_obf_count == 0. Result: pass. Handles medium injection counts (9-49) in repos with no truly dangerous obfuscation. Without dangerous obfuscation to hide payloads, injection pattern matches are FPs from legitimate documentation and tool descriptions.
entries.note: This is the final catch-all before returning None (no auto-clear). Gaps where inj >= 50 AND dangerous_obf == 0 still reach PM review.

entries.title: Auto-clear evaluation order (CRITICAL — order matters)
entries.description: Hard-block check first (dangerous_obf + inj both present). Then: Cat 1 (0 inj + 0 obf) → Cat 2 (obf-only) → Cat 3 (dead) → Cat 10 (PM org) → early exit if inj==0 → Cat 9 (monorepo ratio) → Cat 11 (low inj) → Cat 12 (medium inj, no dangerous obf). If none match, return None = no auto-clear, escalate to PM.

entries.title: Agent skills security profile: obf noise from bundled deps
entries.description: Agent skills (skillsmp source, skill_type=agent_skill) are SKILL.md instruction files. Their repos often include bundled dependencies producing high obfuscation hit counts. This is scanner noise, not a security signal. Primary risk is text-based prompt injection in the instruction content, not code execution. Cat 2 (obf-only, 0 inj) auto-clears these correctly. 128 S-tier agent skills verified, all passed cleanly.
entries.outcome: Cat 2 handles this. No special case needed.

entries.title: Batch 11 supabase monorepo — PM override pattern for large org repos
entries.description: Supabase monorepo: 65 injection patterns across 6,578 files. Cat 9 did not clear (65 > 49 inj threshold). PM investigated and added supabase to PM_VERIFIED_ORGS. Pattern: large established org monorepos with inj > 49 and files > 500 are the edge case that reaches PM review. These are not real threats — PM verifies org legitimacy and adds to Cat 10.
entries.outcome: supabase added to PM_VERIFIED_ORGS; gap: Cat 9 inj<=49 threshold may need raising for very large repos

evolve_history.pm_override_reason: supabase monorepo 65 inj / 6578 files — org added to PM_VERIFIED_ORGS

evolve_history.audit: PM audit — full gap analysis against live code
